message.content:
You are verifier #1, independent. Read the ACTUAL code at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay and decide if this is a TRUE production-readiness problem in current code (6 passes hardened). confirmed=true only if real, reproducible, worth fixing (not speculative/by-design).
J-001: Strict UTF-8 decode in scrubber fails OPEN: one invalid byte routes the whole transcript to verbatim copy, re-leaking secrets/PII
File: scripts/capture_transcripts.py:108-113
VERIFIED. _copy_scrubbed() does `try: text = raw.decode('utf-8') except UnicodeDecodeError: data = raw`. A single invalid UTF-8 byte anywhere in a .jsonl/.json transcript raises UnicodeDecodeError and routes the ENTIRE file to the verbatim branch, copied byte-for-byte with no scrubbing. Claude tool-result transcripts routinely embed arbitrary subprocess stdout (hexdumps, latin-1/non-UTF-8 file reads, truncated multibyte from killed processes) inside JSONL string values, so this is a common condition. Pre-CV-O-1 the .jsonl path decoded with errors='replace' and was ALWAYS scrubbed; the CV-O-1 fix turned an always-redact path into a fail-open one precisely for the files most likely to carry raw dumped data. This is the new code being the defect — the highest-severity privacy guarantee (no operator email/username/path, no credential-shaped secret) now silently fails for affected files.
PoC: src.write_bytes(b'{"out":"[REDACTED] caf\xe9byte=\x80 /Users/[REDACTED]/x"}'); raw=src.read_bytes(); try: data=_scrub(raw.decode('utf-8')).encode() except UnicodeDecodeError: data=raw  ->  b'[REDACTED]' in data == True (LEAKED), b'[REDACTED]' in data == True (LEAKED), b'REDACTED' in data == False. Under pre-CV-O-1 code the same file was decoded errors='replace' and fully scrubbed.
Fix: Decode for scrubbing with errors='replace' (or 'backslashreplace'/'surrogatepass') so a stray byte never disables redaction: `text = raw.decode('utf-8', errors='replace'); data = _scrub(text).encode('utf-8')`. If a genuine binary verbatim path is still wanted, gate it on a real binary signal (`if b'\x00' in raw: data = raw`) rather than on UTF-8 validity, since UTF-8-invalidity != binary. Add a regression test: a .jsonl containing a secret AND an invalid byte must come out scrubbed.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

80	    for pat in _SECRET_PATTERNS:
81	        if pat.groups == 2:
82	            text = pat.sub(r"\1[REDACTED]\2", text)
83	        elif pat.groups == 1:

85	        else:

87	    # Redact the bare operator username everywhere (#G-007), after the path rule so masked paths
88	    # don't re-expose it. Done last so it also catches it inside project-dir slugs / git authors.
89	    if _USERNAME_RE is not None:

91	    return text
92	
93	
94	def _active_session(src: Path) -> Path | None:
95	    """The most-recently-modified top-level transcript = the live session."""
96	    candidates = sorted(src.glob("*.jsonl"), key=lambda p: p.stat().st_mtime, reverse=True)
97	    return candidates[0] if candidates else None
98	
99	
100	def _copy_scrubbed(srcf: Path, dstf: Path) -> dict | None:
101	    try:
102	        dstf.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
103	        raw = srcf.read_bytes()
104	        # Scrub EVERY text file, not only .jsonl (#CV-O-1): the session subtree also holds .json
105	        # workflow/subagent transcripts that carry the same operator PII + credential-shaped
106	        # secrets — the old suffix==".jsonl" gate copied those RAW, bypassing redaction entirely.
107	        # Decode strictly to classify: text → scrub; genuinely binary (rare here) → copy verbatim.
108	        try:
109	            text = raw.decode("utf-8")
110	        except UnicodeDecodeError:
111	            data = raw  # binary blob — nothing to scrub
112	        else:
113	            data = _scrub(text).encode("utf-8")
114	        if dstf.exists() and dstf.read_bytes() == data:
115	            pass  # unchanged — still report it in the manifest
116	        else:
117	            dstf.write_bytes(data)
118	        return {
119	            "path": str(dstf.relative_to(DEST.parent.parent)),
120	            "bytes": len(data),
121	            "sha256": hashlib.sha256(data).hexdigest(),
122	        }
123	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — provenance capture must never break a commit
124	        return None
125	
126	
127	def main() -> int:
128	    src = Path(os.environ.get("ASSAY_TRANSCRIPT_SRC", str(DEFAULT_SRC)))
129	    if not src.is_dir():
130	        return 0
131	    session = _active_session(src)
132	    if session is None:
133	        return 0
134	    sid = session.stem
135	    captured: list[dict] = []
136	
137	    rec = _copy_scrubbed(session, DEST / f"{sid}.jsonl")
138	    if rec:
139	        captured.append(rec)
140	
141	    # the session's subtree: subagents/ + workflows/ transcripts
142	    subtree = src / sid
143	    if subtree.is_dir():
144	        for f in sorted(subtree.rglob("*")):
145	            if f.is_file():
146	                rel = f.relative_to(subtree)
147	                rec = _copy_scrubbed(f, DEST / sid / rel)
148	                if rec:
149	                    captured.append(rec)

content.text: The finding is confirmed exactly as described. Let me verify the PoC behavior.

input.command:
cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && python3 -c "
import re
raw = b'{\"out\":\"[REDACTED] caf\xe9byte=\x80 /Users/[REDACTED]/x\"}'
try:
    text = raw.decode('utf-8')
    print('decoded ok')
except UnicodeDecodeError as e:
    data = raw
    print('UnicodeDecodeError ->', e)
    print('sk-ant in verbatim:', b'[REDACTED]' in data)
    print('[REDACTED] in verbatim:', b'[REDACTED]' in data)
    print('REDACTED in verbatim:', b'REDACTED' in data)
print('---replace path---')
text = raw.decode('utf-8', errors='replace')
print('replace decoded:', repr(text[:40]))
"

content.text:
PoC reproduces exactly. A single invalid UTF-8 byte (0xe9) triggers the except branch, routing the whole file to verbatim copy with secrets and PII intact, no redaction. The `errors='replace'` path decodes successfully so scrubbing can run.

This is a real, reproducible fail-open privacy defect in current code. The verbatim path is gated on UTF-8 validity (not on a genuine binary signal like NUL bytes), and Claude tool-result transcripts routinely embed arbitrary subprocess stdout that can contain non-UTF-8 bytes. The blanket `except Exception` at line 123 also can't catch this since the decode succeeds via the except-UnicodeDecodeError branch and proceeds to write the raw bytes.

Severity: this disables the system's top privacy guarantee (no operator email/username/path, no credential-shaped secret) for exactly the files most likely to carry raw dumped sensitive data. High.

input.reasoning: Confirmed in current code at scripts/capture_transcripts.py:108-113. _copy_scrubbed() decodes raw bytes with strict UTF-8: `try: text = raw.decode('utf-8') except UnicodeDecodeError: data = raw`. On any UnicodeDecodeError the entire file is assigned to the verbatim branch (data = raw) and written byte-for-byte with zero scrubbing. The else branch (scrubbing via _scrub) only runs when decode fully succeeds. Reproduced the PoC: a JSONL value containing `[REDACTED]...`, `/Users/[REDACTED]/x`, plus one invalid byte (0xe9) raises UnicodeDecodeError, so the file is copied verbatim with `[REDACTED]` present (True), `[REDACTED]` present (True), and no `REDACTED` (False). The `errors='replace'` decode succeeds and lets _scrub run normally. The outer `except Exception` at line 123 does not catch this because the decode-error is handled internally and execution proceeds to write raw bytes. The defect is real because: (1) it is the new CV-O-1 code that introduced fail-open behavior (per finding, the pre-CV-O-1 .jsonl path used errors='replace' and always scrubbed); (2) the verbatim path is gated on UTF-8 validity rather than a real binary signal (NUL byte), and UTF-8-invalid != binary; (3) Claude tool-result transcripts commonly embed arbitrary subprocess stdout (hexdumps, latin-1 reads, truncated multibyte) inside JSONL strings, making invalid bytes a realistic, common condition; and (4) it silently disables the system's primary privacy guarantee for precisely the files most likely to carry raw sensitive data. Fix is sound: decode with errors='replace' before scrubbing, and if a binary verbatim path is still desired, gate it on `b'\x00' in raw` rather than UTF-8 validity; add a regression test (jsonl with a secret + an invalid byte must come out scrubbed).

content.text: Confirmed J-001 as a true high-severity fail-open privacy defect. The strict UTF-8 decode at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/scripts/capture_transcripts.py:108-113 routes any file with a single invalid byte to verbatim copy, bypassing all secret/PII redaction. Reproduced the PoC: secrets and operator paths survive unredacted.
